title: Lane 3 coastal corridor spatial depth
summary: Deepen the beach, coastal scrub, and forest-edge physical transition without route, station, or journal dependencies.

execution_order.goal: Scope one coastal corridor spatial-depth pass with local proof.
execution_order.why_now: The front-half route now has a trailhead anchor and can support a slightly richer physical transition.

execution_order.goal: Implement the scoped coastal corridor spatial pass.
execution_order.why_now: The coast-to-forest walk should feel connected before broader beta routing expands.

execution_order.goal: Review spatial depth for readability, prompt competition, and science-safe carriers.
execution_order.why_now: Spatial richness must remain playable at 256x160.

completion_gate: One coastal/forest-edge spatial improvement lands or is declined by proof.
Browser proof is captured if geometry or placement changes land.
No station, route-board, route catalog, save schema, or physics rewrite lands.
guardrails: Do not create a new traversal framework.
post_packet_path: If clean, proceed to forest expedition spatial depth.
If a route need appears, hand it to lane 4.

scout_493_handoff.proof_summary: Beach-to-scrub corridor and Forest Trail arrival already read at native 256x160. The coastal-to-forest corridor center has science-safe carriers but no physical hold, so it reads flat compared with the surrounding authored route.
main_493_contract: Touch only src/engine/corridor.ts and src/test/corridor.test.ts unless fresh proof shows a directly local need.
Add one tiny authored root/log hold inside COASTAL_TO_FOREST_CORRIDOR_ID around the existing salmonberry/nurse-log transition.
Use existing platform sprites and existing carriers only; do not add new entries or route beats.
Keep the left lip on the coastal-scrub-owned half and place the rest near or just across the threshold so the corridor remains exit-safe into Forest Trail.
Capture fresh native 256x160 browser proof for the coastal-forest center hold and Forest Trail arrival after the geometry change.
out_of_scope: Station shell, route-board logic, route catalog semantics, save schema, traversal helpers, physics rewrites, new route beats, new carriers, and new biome or cave systems.
Beach-to-scrub corridor geometry, Coastal Scrub forest-edge world geometry, and Forest Trail trailhead geometry unless fresh proof shows a local regression.

runtime_changes: Added sheltered-edge-root-lip and sheltered-edge-log-rest inside COASTAL_TO_FOREST_CORRIDOR_ID.
Used existing log-platform sprite and existing corridor carriers only.
Added focused corridor test coverage for the new threshold hold.
main_493_result.proof_summary: Fresh native 256x160 proof confirms the new sheltered-edge hold is visible in the coastal-forest corridor center with nearbyTravelTarget null, and the corridor still exits into Forest Trail trailhead.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "walks the full adjacent corridor chain"
local browser canvas proof at native 256x160

critic_493_review.summary: The coastal-to-forest corridor hold is local, readable at native 256x160, uses existing sprites and carriers only, and keeps the corridor exit-safe with no travel-prompt competition inside the corridor.
verification_reviewed: Proof assertions over output/lane-3-main-493-coastal-corridor-spatial-proof/summary.json, assertions.json, and errors.json
npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "walks the full adjacent corridor chain"
Implementation report verification: npm test -- --run src/test/corridor.test.ts; npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "walks the full adjacent corridor chain"; npm run build
